tests.name: Test de Ansiedad Generalizada (GAD-7)
tests.description: Evalúa síntomas de trastorno de ansiedad generalizada en las últimas 2 semanas
tests.disclaimerBefore: Este cuestionario es solo educativo y NO es un diagnóstico clínico. Sus resultados son para reflexión personal. Si tienes dudas o síntomas graves, consulta con un profesional de la salud. Tus respuestas se guardan solo en tu navegador.
tests.disclaimerAfter: Este test NO es diagnóstico clínico y NO reemplaza la evaluación de un profesional. Si tienes síntomas graves o persistentes, consulta con tu médico o psicólogo.

questions.text: ¿Con qué frecuencia te has sentido nervioso/a, ansioso/a o tenso/a?
¿Con qué frecuencia no has podido dejar de preocuparte o controlar tus preocupaciones?
¿Con qué frecuencia te has preocupado demasiado por cosas diferentes?
¿Con qué frecuencia has tenido dificultad para relajarte?
¿Con qué frecuencia te has sentido tan inquieto/a que te cuesta estar sentado/a?
¿Con qué frecuencia te has irritado o molestado fácilmente?
¿Con qué frecuencia has tenido miedo de que ocurra algo terrible?

tests.name: Cuestionario de Salud del Paciente (PHQ-9)
tests.description: Evalúa síntomas de depresión en las últimas 2 semanas. Basado en Kroenke, Spitzer & Williams (2001).
tests.disclaimerBefore: Este cuestionario es solo educativo y NO es un diagnóstico clínico. Sus resultados son para reflexión personal. Si tienes dudas o síntomas graves, consulta con un profesional de la salud. Tus respuestas se guardan solo en tu navegador.
tests.disclaimerAfter: Este test NO es diagnóstico clínico y NO reemplaza la evaluación de un profesional. Si tienes síntomas graves o persistentes, consulta con tu médico o psicólogo. Basado en Kroenke, Spitzer & Williams (2001).

questions.text: ¿Con qué frecuencia te has sentido sin interés o placer en hacer las cosas?
¿Con qué frecuencia te has sentido decaído/a, deprimido/a o sin esperanza?
¿Con qué frecuencia has tenido dificultad para dormir, mantenerte dormido/a o has dormido demasiado?
¿Con qué frecuencia te has sentido cansado/a o con poca energía?
¿Con qué frecuencia has tenido poco apetito o has comido en exceso?
¿Con qué frecuencia te has sentido mal contigo mismo/a, que eres un/a fracasado/a, o que has fallado a ti o a tu familia?
¿Con qué frecuencia has tenido dificultad para concentrarte en cosas como leer el periódico o ver la televisión?
¿Con qué frecuencia te has movido o hablado tan despacio que los demás lo han notado? ¿O lo contrario, has estado tan inquieto/a o agitado/a que te has movido mucho más de lo normal?
¿Con qué frecuencia has pensado que estarías mejor muerto/a, o has tenido pensamientos de hacerte daño de alguna manera?

tests.name: Generalized Anxiety Disorder Scale (GAD-7)
tests.description: Assesses symptoms of generalized anxiety disorder over the past 2 weeks. Based on Spitzer et al., 2006.
tests.disclaimerBefore: This questionnaire is for educational purposes only and is NOT a clinical diagnosis. Results are for personal reflection. If you have concerns or severe symptoms, please consult a healthcare professional. Your answers are stored only in your browser.
tests.disclaimerAfter: This test is NOT a clinical diagnosis and does NOT replace evaluation by a professional. If you have severe or persistent symptoms, please consult your doctor or psychologist.

questions.text: How often have you been feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge?
How often have you been unable to stop or control worrying?
How often have you been worrying too much about different things?
How often have you had trouble relaxing?
How often have you been so restless that it was hard to sit still?
How often have you become easily annoyed or irritable?
How often have you felt afraid as if something awful might happen?

tests.name: Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ-9)
tests.description: Assesses symptoms of depression over the past 2 weeks. Based on Kroenke, Spitzer & Williams (2001).
tests.disclaimerBefore: This questionnaire is for educational purposes only and is NOT a clinical diagnosis. Results are for personal reflection. If you have concerns or severe symptoms, please consult a healthcare professional. Your answers are stored only in your browser.
tests.disclaimerAfter: This test is NOT a clinical diagnosis and does NOT replace evaluation by a professional. If you have severe or persistent symptoms, please consult your doctor or psychologist. Based on Kroenke, Spitzer & Williams (2001).

questions.text: How often have you had little interest or pleasure in doing things?
How often have you been feeling down, depressed, or hopeless?
How often have you had trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much?
How often have you been feeling tired or having little energy?
How often have you had poor appetite or been overeating?
How often have you been feeling bad about yourself — or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down?
How often have you had trouble concentrating on things, such as reading or watching television?
How often have you been moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed? Or the opposite — being so fidgety or restless that you have been moving around much more than usual?
How often have you had thoughts that you would be better off dead, or thoughts of hurting yourself in some way?
